The first installment in the Cotton series, players assume the role of the young witch Cotton who, alongside her fairy companion Silk, sets out on her broomstick on a quest to defeat several monsters and get her Willow candy. Its gameplay mainly consists of shooting mixed with role-playing elements using a main two-button configuration. It ran on the Sega System 16 hardware.
Cotton was later ported to multiple platforms, each one featuring several changes or additions compared with the original version. Cotton proved to be popular among players in Japanese arcades, earning several awards from Gamest magazine; however, the console ports have been met with mixed reception from critics. It is regarded as a key title in the "cute 'em up" sub-genre. On November 28, 2024, the arcade version of the game was ported as part of the Arcade Archives series.

title_jp: クレイジー・ホスピタル ふしぎの国の天使
overview_en: Unofficial adult themed digital comic.
overview_zh: 非官方的成人主題數位漫畫。
no_intro_name: Crazy Hospital - Fushigi no Kuni no Tenshi (Japan)

title_jp: クレスト・オブ・ウルフ狼的紋章
overview_en: One of the few scrolling beat 'em ups on the PC Engine, Crest of Wolf will be very familiar to fans of Final Fight. The standard rescue-the-girl plot line is here, giving you a choice of two characters - Hawk and Tony. The moves are fairly typical for a game of this genre, with normal punches, grab-and-throw when you get close, and a life sapping special attack for those times when you are feeling a little overwhelmed by the bad guys. Each level has many sub-sections and ends in a boss fight.
overview_zh: Crest of Wolf 是 PC Engine 上少數的橫向捲動格鬥遊戲之一，《Final Fight》的粉絲會對它感到非常熟悉。標準的「救女友」劇情線在此呈現，可從兩位角色中選擇——Hawk 和 Tony。招式設定相當典型，具備普通拳擊、近距離時的抓取與投技，以及在被壞蛋壓著打時可以用的減血特殊攻擊。每一關分為多個段落，結尾都有王者戰。
comment_en: This scrolling beat em up certainly shows the PC Engine is technically capable of moving big sprites around with no clash or slowdown. Unfortunately the graphics are  rough and look almost half-finished, although this is partly down to the 'style' it just looks ugly in places and the lack of colours used on the background doesn't help matters. The sprites are reminiscent of Final Fight, but are poorly proportioned and badly animated for the most part. Visually, it's a disappointment coming from a developer like Hudson. Music is neither great nor poor - there are a few nice tracks and sound effects are sampled, which is essential to get that feel of proper impact. The game itself is simplistic and repetitive with a lack of moves to add variety and the levels are a little too long, although the frequent change of scenery is welcome. It isn't all bad - the game is fun for a while and if you are in the mood for something long and brainless then it serves its purpose well. But you can't help thinking that with a little more time and love put into it, it could have been a stand-out game for the machine. sunteam_paul Turbo Views Review
comment_zh: 這款橫向捲動格鬥遊戲確實展現出 PC Engine 在技術上能處理大量大型角色精靈、不衝突也不掉幀。可惜畫面粗糙、看起來像半完成品——雖然部分是「風格」使然，但某些地方就是醜，而且背景配色偏少也沒有幫助。角色精靈讓人聯想到《Final Fight》，但比例不協調、多數動畫粗糙。視覺上對出自 Hudson 這種開發商的作品來說令人失望。音樂不算出色也不算差——有幾首不錯的曲子，音效是採樣，帶出應有的打擊感。遊戲本身簡單而重複，招式少缺乏變化，關卡也有點過長，儘管場景變換頻繁還算不錯。不全是缺點——玩一下挺好玩的，如果你想來點耗時又不用動腦的東西，它還算夠格。但你忍不住會想，只要再多花點時間與用心，它本可以是這部主機上的代表作。sunteam_paul Turbo Views Review

overview_en: An upgrade to the previous Cyber Cross, Cross Wiber provides similar platform fighting gameplay with various Power Rangers style transformations, but also throws in some side scrolling shoot 'em up action for a change of pace.
overview_zh: Cross Wiber 是前作 Cyber Cross 的升級版，提供類似的平台動作格鬥玩法，搭配各種《金剛戰士》風格的變身，還加入了橫向捲動射擊段落以增添節奏變化。

title_jp: サイバーシティ オーエド 808獣の属性
overview_en: Cyberpunk style adventure game set in a dystopian world, adapted from the anime series. Images Front Cover  1 • Front Cover 2 • Front Cover 3  • Back Cover • Spine • CD • Reg Card Front • Reg Card Back
overview_zh: 改編自同名動畫系列的賽博龐克風格冒險遊戲，背景設定在反烏托邦世界。圖片：正面封面 1・正面封面 2・正面封面 3・背面封面・書背・CD・登錄卡正面・登錄卡背面
no_intro_name: Cyber City Oedo 808 - Kemono no Alignment (Japan)

overview_en: Battle ferocious giant insects in your 'craft' that has various insect-like forms that you can switch between by collecting coloured drops.
overview_zh: 駕駛你的「載具」與兇猛的巨型昆蟲戰鬥——載具有多種昆蟲形態，可以透過收集彩色液滴來切換。
wiki_extract_en:
Cyber Core is a 1990 vertically scrolling shooter video game developed by Alfa System and published in Japan by Information Global Service (IGS) and in North America by NEC for the TurboGrafx-16. Set in the year 2269 where Earth has been overrun by an alien race known as Hyper Insects, the player controls a Chimera bio-fighter craft, piloted by the enforcer Rad Ralph (Kato Melange in the Japanese version) in order to fend off the invaders and reclaim the planet. Similar to Dragon Spirit, Ralph has a projectile weapon for destroying air-based enemies and a bomb for destroying ground-based enemies.
Co-headed by director Kenji Hisatomi and producer Kotoshi Yokoyama, Cyber Core was the first shoot 'em up title created by Alfa System, who would later work on future projects such as Down Load and the Shikigami no Shiro series. First released on the TurboGrafx-16, the game was ported to the X68000 by SPS, featuring improved visuals and audio, as well as a rebalanced difficulty. The X68000 conversion has since been re-released only in Japan for Microsoft Windows through D4 Enterprise's Project EGG download service.
Cyber Core on the TurboGrafx-16 received mostly positive reception from critics, but the X68000 conversion was met with mixed reception; reviewers drew comparisons with Dragon Spirit due to its gameplay system but praise was given to its technical performance, visual presentation, sound, and fast-paced gameplay, but criticism was geared towards the repetitive action and enemy attack patterns, while many were divided in regards to its difficulty.

title_jp: 武装刑事サイバークロス
overview_en: Rolling Thunder-esque platform beat 'em up which gives you the ability to transform into a brightly coloured Power Suit as you fight crime on the streets.
overview_zh: 類似《Rolling Thunder》的平台格鬥遊戲，你可以變身成色彩鮮豔的 Power Suit，在街頭打擊犯罪。
wiki_extract_en: Cyber Cross: Busou Keiji is a beat 'em up video game released for the PC Engine in 1989, developed by Face Corporation. It has a sequel called Cross Wiber.

overview_en: Futuristic dodgeball game with a choice of fantastical teams (some suspiciously like certain famous movies) and a varied collection of themed courts.
overview_zh: 未來風格的躲避球遊戲，可選擇多支奇幻隊伍（有些跟知名電影有可疑的相似之處），以及主題多樣的各種球場。

overview_en: Sci-fi role playing game combining traditional overhead view and turn-based combat with very slight digital-comic undertones.
overview_zh: 科幻角色扮演遊戲，結合了傳統俯視視角與回合制戰鬥，還帶有少許數位漫畫的氛圍。
wiki_extract_en: Cyber Knight (サイバーナイト, Saibā Naito) is a science fiction PC Engine and Super Famicom role-playing video game that combines sci-fi space exploration with strategic robot combat. This game is intended for experienced role-playing gamers only. The random encounters are high even for a Japanese role-playing game with a mid-to-late 1992 Super Famicom release date (e.g., Dragon Quest V: Hand of the Heavenly Bride) and the combat sequences (using melee attacks in addition to missiles and guns) are extremely slow. Two years later, a sequel was released for this game titled Cyber Knight II: Chikyū Teikoku no Yabō. The games were only released commercially in Japan, however there are a fan-translations from Japanese to English by Aeon Genesis.

overview_en: Based on the legend of the Monkey King, this takes the shoot 'em up genre and puts it into a fantastical ancient Chinese setting. Riding on your cloud, you have to fend off oriental spirits and monsters that seem to have taken a dislike for you. Take care though, as one hit and you are dead - sending you back to the last restart point in the level. Helping you on your travels are, of course, the obligatory power-up pods and also an occasional shop where you can purchase such useful powers as a ring of fire and 3-way reverse shots. Also handy is the fact that you can spin around at any time to shoot whatever is creeping up from behind. At varying stages throughout each level you face a boss which must be dispatched to move on.
overview_zh: 本作以《西遊記》的美猴王傳說為靈感，將射擊遊戲類型帶入奇幻的古代中國場景。乘坐在雲朵上的你，必須擊退看你不順眼的東方妖怪與神魔。不過要小心，被打到一下就掛了——只能回到關卡上一個重生點。在你旅途中協助你的當然有必備的強化道具，還有不定期出現的商店，可以在其中購買火焰圈、三向反射彈等實用能力。另一個好用的機制是你可以隨時轉身，射擊背後偷襲你的敵人。每關中段都會遇上王者，必須擊敗才能前進。

aka: LEGEND OF HIDDEN TREASURE - CHRIS'S ADVENTURE
overview_en: A platform game with hints of both Indiana Jones and Ghouls 'n' Ghosts, Hiho Densetsu puts you in the shoes of Chris, a young woman travelling the globe. Deadly enemies burst from the ground, traps and moving platforms must be negotiated and undead skeletons leap down from the ceiling and all you have to protect yourself is a short knife. But don't fear, as collecting coloured orbs increases the power of your weapon, allowing bolts of energy to be fired from the tip. Each level is divided into stages, but keep an eye on the sky as you only have a short amount of time to complete each one.
overview_zh: 帶有《印第安納瓊斯》和《Ghouls 'n' Ghosts》影子的平台動作遊戲，Hiho Densetsu 讓你扮演環遊世界的年輕女性 Chris。致命的敵人從地底冒出、陷阱與移動平台要跨越、不死骷髏從天花板撲下，而你只有一把短刀防身。但別害怕，收集彩色能量球能提升武器威力，讓刀尖射出能量彈。每一關分成若干段，但要留意天空，因為每一段都有時間限制。

title_jp: カトちゃんケンちゃん
overview_en: Based on a popular comedy duo, Hudson has taken the gameplay from the original Wonderboy and transplanted it with a dash of toilet humour. Many key elements from Wonderboy are here...your ever decreasing health bar (topped up by fruit), the method to jump higher by holding down the attack button, but there are a few differences. Attacks are made in several ways; first by a simple kick (short range and difficult to judge), second by a powerful bottom burp (but remember to turn around before letting one rip) and if those are too much, just jump on the enemies heads in a Mario style. Throughout the landscapes are many objects to be kicked, some gain you coins, some fruit, others open up bonus areas (like the Wonderboy-style bonus level in the clouds). Also, kicking a doorway will open it, allowing you to venture inside to either full health and tips from your partner (usually in some crazy disguise) or a fruit machine where you can gamble collected coins to gain goodies. And speaking of your partner, look out for him along the way, often making a nuisance of himself by throwing cans at you or taking a dump behind bushes.
overview_zh: 本作以當紅搞笑搭檔為藍本，Hudson 將原版《Wonderboy》的玩法移植過來，還添加了一些廁所幽默。《Wonderboy》的許多關鍵元素都在……持續下降的血條（靠水果補血）、按住攻擊鍵跳得更高的手法都一樣，但也有些差異。攻擊方式有幾種——首先是簡單的踢擊（短距離而且難以判定），其次是威力強大的放屁（記得先轉身再放），如果這些都嫌麻煩，也可以用 Mario 風格直接跳到敵人頭上。場景中有許多可踢的物品，有些能獲得硬幣、有些是水果，有些則會開啟隱藏區域（例如《Wonderboy》風格的雲端隱藏關卡）。另外，踢門可以打開，進去有時能全補血並聽搭檔給予提示（他通常會穿奇怪的裝扮），有時則會看到吃角子老虎，可以用收集到的硬幣賭博獲取好物。說到搭檔，沿途要小心他，他常常會來搗亂——丟罐頭砸你或躲在灌木叢後面大便。
wiki_extract_en:
Kato-chan Ken-chan Gokigen TV (加トちゃんケンちゃんごきげんテレビ, Kato-chan Ken-chan Gokigen Terebi) is a Japanese television variety show that aired on Tokyo Broadcasting System around the mid-1980s. Starring Ken Shimura and Cha Kato, former members of the group The Drifters from Hachiji Dayo! Zen'in Shugo, the irreverent and satirical program would poke fun at contemporary society in Japan, and would feature comedy vignettes similar to those found on The Benny Hill Show or The Carol Burnett Show. Leslie Nielsen once made a special appearance on the show as well.
The duo also had a PC Engine video game, Kato-chan and Ken-chan, based on their antics, filled with toilet humor, and featuring them as playable characters. It was translated and censored for a Western TurboGrafx-16 release as J.J. & Jeff due to copyright issues and the toilet humor.

title_jp: カルメンサンディエゴを追え！世界編
overview_en: Educational travel adventure game.
overview_zh: 教育性質的環遊世界冒險遊戲。
no_intro_name: Where in the World Is Carmen Sandiego (Japan) (En,Ja)
wiki_extract_en:
Where in the World Is Carmen Sandiego? is an educational video game released by Broderbund on April 23, 1985. It is the first product in the Carmen Sandiego franchise. The game was distributed with The World Almanac and Book of Facts, published by Pharos Books. An enhanced version of the game was released in 1989, which did not have the almanac-based copy protection and instead used disk-based copy protection. A deluxe version was released in 1990, and features additional animation and a reworked interface from the original version. Some of the bonus features include digitized photos from National Geographic, over 3200 clues, music from the Smithsonian/Folkways Recordings, 20 villains, 60 countries, and 16 maps. CD-ROM versions for MS-DOS and Mac were released in 1992. A Windows version was released in 1994.
In the game, the player takes the role of a rookie in the ACME Detective Agency, tasked to track down crooks from the V.I.L.E. organization who have stolen famous works from around the world. They do this by using their knowledge of geography (aided by the Almanac) to question witnesses or investigate clues to track down where the crook has gone. Successfully solving these crimes increases the player's rank in ACME, leading to more difficult cases and later being tasked with finding the leader of V.I.L.E. and namesake for the game, Carmen Sandiego.
The game was initially developed as a menu-driven interface to replace the text-driven interface of adventure games like Colossal Cave Adventure for graphic-enabled computers like the Apple II. Along the way, the idea of introducing geography as part of the game and distributing the Almanac with the game shifted its approach. While it was not intended as an educational game at release, the game proved very successful as an educational tool for schools. By 1995, over four million copies of the game had been sold, and established the Carmen Sandiego franchise. This game is not to be confused with the 1996 rebooted version sometimes mistakenly called the "Deluxe" version.
wiki_title: Where in the World Is Carmen Sandiego? (1985 video game)

title_en: AKUMAJŌ DRACULA X - CHI NO RONDO
aka: DRACULA X - RONDO OF BLOOD, CASTLEVANIA
overview_en: Taking the role of Richter Belmont, you set out to destroy the evil presence of Dracula. You make your way into Dracula's castle armed only with your whip - but along the way you can pick up secondary weapons, such as throwing axes, daggers and time-stop watches. Secondary weapons are powered by collecting hearts - each also has a super power that can be activated when you are really up a creek without a paddle. The levels are split into various sections and end with a boss fight. However, there are multiple routes through most levels that allow you to open up different areas to play - this means that you can complete the game without playing every level, giving the player incentive to replay to find all the secrets.
overview_zh: 扮演 Richter Belmont，你將出發消滅德古拉的邪惡存在。你只帶著鞭子闖進德古拉的城堡——但沿路可撿到次要武器，例如投擲斧、匕首和時停懷錶。次要武器需要收集愛心才能使用，每種都還有在走投無路時可發動的超必殺。關卡分成多個段落，以王者戰收尾。不過大多數關卡都有多條路線，可以開啟不同區域遊玩——這表示你無需通過每一關就能破台，給玩家再玩一次去尋找所有秘密的動機。
comment_en: Dracula X is an action platform game of the highest quality. The visuals are superb throughout with some great parallax scrolling, huge sprites and atmospheric design. The music is also top notch with familiar tunes remixed well and some great new music too. The gameplay is balanced carefully - it's quite challenging in places, but practice always pays off without it getting too frustrating. This game is highly regarded among both PC Engine collectors and Dracula/Castlevania fans alike - it is one of the finest entries in the series (before it took on Metroid-style gameplay) and an essential purchase. sunteam_paul
comment_zh: Dracula X 是一款最高等級的動作平台遊戲。視覺表現全程出色，視差捲動極棒、角色精靈巨大、氣氛設計到位。音樂同樣頂級，熟悉的曲目重新混音得很好，也加入了許多出色的新曲。玩法平衡拿捏得恰到好處——某些地方雖然有難度，但練習總能突破，不會讓人過於氣餒。這款遊戲在 PC Engine 收藏家與德古拉/惡魔城系列粉絲中都備受推崇——它是系列中最優秀的作品之一（在系列轉為 Metroid 風格玩法之前），是必入手的佳作。sunteam_paul
no_intro_name: Akumajou Dracula X - Chi no Rondo (Japan)

overview_en: Take control of a team of animals as you plant crops and fight back invaders.
overview_zh: 操控一支動物團隊，一邊種植作物、一邊擊退入侵者。

overview_en: Vertically scrolling shoot 'em up, graphically reminiscent of Raiden, with a World War II setting. You set out over various enemy landscapes destroying planes, tanks and boats. Power-ups give you increased weapon damage and you also have a special weapon in the form of a squadron of fighters that comes to your aid.
overview_zh: 縱向捲動射擊遊戲，畫面風格讓人聯想起《Raiden（雷電）》，背景設定為二戰時期。你會飛越各種敵方地形，擊毀飛機、坦克與船艦。強化道具會增加武器傷害，另外還有特殊武器——一個會前來支援你的戰機中隊。
comment_en: Daisenpu is another in the long list of vertical shoot 'em ups for the PC Engine, and to make its mark it really needs to stand out from the others. Graphically, it has a similar style to other Toaplan shooters and ends up looking  like Raiden and Kyukyoku Tiger, if a little more basic in places. But generally the art is nice, especially things like broken bridges and derelict buildings. Sonically, it fares less well. The music starts off into a fast beat that puts you in the mood for some action, but then just seems to repeat itself with very little variation. It does the job if you are concentrating on playing, but it would have been nice to have had something with a better composition. Gameplay is where Daisenpu starts to show its cracks. All the enemies are ground based - tanks, vans, bigger tanks, boats, even bigger tanks. While this is an interesting design choice to make, it does hinder the variety, particularly compared with similar games. It's not the fastest paced of shooters, but the action is fairly continuous and it doesn't seem to have any low spots in the levels (which flow into each other without a break - like one continuous level). But the problem is that they just aren't that interesting. On the plus side, the restart points can be quite generous (even skipping some minor bosses that you failed to defeat), which is nice because dodging bullets seems to need a lot more care than in other shooters. In all, I'd call it 'solid' rather than 'spectacular'. There's something inherently dull about it but it should scratch the shoot 'em up itch if you're eager for something new to play. An interesting note is that this version is superior to the CD version, which has cut-down graphics and some areas  missing. sunteam_paul
comment_zh: Daisenpu 是 PC Engine 上眾多縱向射擊遊戲中的又一款，要留下印象就必須從眾多作品中脫穎而出。畫面風格與 Toaplan 的其他射擊作品類似，最後看起來像《Raiden》和《Kyukyoku Tiger》，某些地方略為簡陋。但整體而言美術不錯，特別是斷橋和廢棄建築之類的細節。音樂方面就比較遜色。開場音樂是快節奏，讓你進入戰鬥情緒，但之後就一直重複，變化不多。專心打遊戲時還勉強可以，但如果配樂能多下點功夫就更好了。玩法部分 Daisenpu 就開始露出瑕疵。所有敵人都是地面單位——坦克、卡車、更大的坦克、船、更更大的坦克。雖然這是個有趣的設計選擇，但確實限制了變化性，尤其是跟類似遊戲相比。節奏不算是最快的射擊遊戲，但動作相當連貫，關卡中沒有明顯的低潮（關卡之間無中斷、流暢銜接，像是一個連續的關卡）。但問題是這些關卡就是不夠有趣。好的方面是重生點相當慷慨（甚至會跳過你沒打敗的小王者），這很好，因為躲子彈所需的精細度比其他射擊遊戲還高。總結來說，我會稱它「紮實」而非「出色」。它有種難以言喻的平淡感，但若你急需新遊戲解癮，這款能搔到射擊迷的癢處。值得一提的是，此版本比 CD 版本更優秀——CD 版畫面有所縮減、某些區域也遺漏了。sunteam_paul

overview_en: CD version of Toaplan's World War II fighter plane shooter. Despite being on CD, this is inferior to the HuCARD version.
overview_zh: Toaplan 二戰戰鬥機射擊遊戲的 CD 版本。儘管是 CD 版，卻不如 HuCard卡匣版本。

overview_en: Hex-based strategy war game set in modern times. Up to four players can compete in battles set in various different countries. Images Front Cover • Back Cover • Manual Front Cover • CD  • Print Ad
overview_zh: 以現代為背景的六角格戰略戰爭遊戲。最多四人可以在不同國家的戰場中對戰。圖片：正面封面・背面封面・說明書正面・CD・印刷廣告

overview_en: Only around 800 produced, Darius Alpha was specially produced and is a straight run through of only the bosses from Darius Plus, making it purely a collectors item.
overview_zh: 僅約 800 份生產量，Darius Alpha 是特別製作的版本，內容只是連續打 Darius Plus 的所有王者，純粹是收藏品。

overview_en: A conversion of Taito's multi-screen arcade shoot 'em up, Darius Plus features a choice of paths through the game after defeating each giant aquatic-style boss. Gameplay is fairly traditional with power-ups increasing the damage of your forward shots and bombs.
overview_zh: 移植自 Taito 多螢幕大型電玩射擊遊戲的作品，Darius Plus 在每個巨大水生風格王者擊敗後可選擇不同路線。玩法相當傳統，強化道具會提升前向射擊與炸彈的傷害。
comment_en: Darius Plus is a nice, but drawn out, horizontally scrolling shoot ‘em up. Darius has always had a style of it’s own and that is much of where its appeal lays. The bosses are very original and have some quite impressive detail and the levels are generally  attractive, if repetitive. The only trouble with the Darius games is that once you lose your weapons, you may as well kiss your whole game goodbye as it can be very difficult to recover. So one for the die hard shoot ‘em up fans only. A special note should go to the music which is unusual and a bit of an acquired taste, but great. sunteam_paul
comment_zh: Darius Plus 是款不錯但有點冗長的橫向捲動射擊遊戲。Darius 一向有自己的風格，這也是它吸引人的地方之一。王者設計非常原創，細節相當令人印象深刻，關卡整體畫面吸引人，雖然略顯重複。Darius 系列唯一的問題是一旦失去武器，你幾乎可以跟整局遊戲說再見了，因為恢復非常困難。所以只推薦給死忠射擊迷。特別提一下音樂——它很特別，屬於要慢慢習慣的類型，但很棒。sunteam_paul

overview_en: American made platform game based on a Disney property. Turbo Views Review
overview_zh: 改編自 Disney 作品的美製平台遊戲。Turbo Views Review
wiki_extract_en: Darkwing Duck is a platform video game developed and published by Capcom for the Nintendo Entertainment System in 1992. It was based on the Disney animated television series Darkwing Duck. It was ported to the Game Boy in 1993.

overview_en: Digital comic style adventure game.
overview_zh: 數位漫畫風格的冒險遊戲。

overview_en: Spaceship based shoot 'em up that takes you from Earth to various alien landscapes to defeat the enemy.
overview_zh: 以太空船為主題的射擊遊戲，帶你從地球飛越各種外星地貌，打敗敵人。
comment_en: While being yet another horizontally scrolling shoot ‘em up on the Engine, this one has a few points to recommend it. Presentation and graphics are generally excellent, with plenty of parallax giving a real feeling of depth, and the game keeps you interested for long enough to make it worth a try. Although it’s not an all time classic, it’s put together so well it’s  worth the effort. sunteam_paul Turbo Views Review
comment_zh: 雖然是 PC Engine 上又一款橫向捲動射擊遊戲，這款仍有幾個值得推薦的特色。呈現與畫面整體優秀，大量視差帶來真正的空間感，遊戲也讓你維持夠長的興趣，值得一試。雖然稱不上全時代經典，但製作相當用心，值得一玩。sunteam_paul Turbo Views Review
wiki_extract_en: Dead Moon (デッドムーン 月世界の悪夢, Dead Moon: Tsuki Sekai no Akumu; lit. "Dead Moon: Nightmare of the Moon World") is a horizontally scrolling shooter released for the TurboGrafx-16. It was developed by Zap and published by TSS on February 22, 1991 in Japan and later in 1992 in North America. It was re-released for the Wii Virtual Console in 2007 by Natsume Inc.

title_jp: デッド・オブ・ザ・ブレイン１＆２
overview_en: The final official PC Engine release (amazingly in 1999!), Dead of the Brain 1 & 2 are conversions of digital comics, strongly influenced by zombie movies.
overview_zh: PC Engine 的最後一款官方作品（驚人地在 1999 年推出！），Dead of the Brain 1 & 2 是數位漫畫的移植作品，深受殭屍電影影響。
wiki_extract_en:
Dead of the Brain is a Japanese horror-themed adventure game, developed by FairyTale and released in March 1992. The player controls a man named Cole amidst a zombie outbreak, arisen from his friend's resurrection experiment gone wrong.
FairyTale had, following an incident regarding their erotic game Saori (1991), switched to developing horror video games which began with Dead of the Brain. Two other games were released as part of the Nightmare Collection: the game's sequel Dead of the Brain 2 (1993) and Marine Philt (1993). Following these releases, the company returned to developing eroge.
A port of Dead of the Brain was bundled with its sequel to the PC Engine CD. While originally planned for release in January 1996, the bundle only released in  1999, making it the final official PC Engine game.

title_jp: デスブリンガー THE KNIGHT OF DARKNESS
overview_en: Fantasy-based RPG.
overview_zh: 奇幻主題 RPG。
no_intro_name: Death Bringer - The Knight of Darkness (Japan)

overview_en: Promotional version of Bomber Man featuring 'Kabuki' from the Far East of Eden series.
overview_zh: 《Bomber Man》的宣傳版本，主角是《天外魔境（Far East of Eden）》系列中的「歌舞伎」。
no_intro_name: Tengai Makyou - Deden no Den - Kabuki (Hudson) (Japan)

title_jp: ディープブルー・海底神話
overview_en: There are many shooters on the PC Engine, but not that many set underwater. Deep Blue puts you in control of a fish-shaped submersible of some sort, complete with cannon to blast away any nasty fishy types that get in your way. And believe me, there are a LOT of these angry denizens of the deep that would like nothing more than to take you down. As with most shooters, the occasional power-up comes your way when you shoot a particular type of fish, and these give you bigger shots, a long purple shot, full energy and so on. The large eye on your fishy vehicle shows your energy status and any collisions will deplete this pretty fast - as you only have a single life you have to be  nimble to survive.
overview_zh: PC Engine 上的射擊遊戲很多，但水下場景的並不常見。Deep Blue 讓你操控某種魚形潛艇，裝配大砲，射擊擋路的討厭魚類。相信我，水底深處有一大堆憤怒的生物巴不得把你幹掉。跟大多數射擊遊戲一樣，擊中特定類型的魚會偶爾掉出強化道具，提供更大的射擊、長長的紫色光束、滿血量等效果。你魚形載具上的大眼睛顯示能量狀態，任何碰撞都會迅速消耗能量——由於你只有一條命，必須靈巧才能存活。
comment_en: Pack In Video are not known for making great games. In fact, they're known for making pretty damn awful games, and Deep Blue is just one of the reasons why. Almost legendarily awful, why anyone would want to play this game more than once is beyond me. The graphics are bland with seemingly no variety in the forever-scrolling background, the music grinds on you after a while and the gameplay is shocking. Enemies shoot around the screen in such zig-zaggy patterns that it is almost impossible to avoid getting hit every few seconds, you can only shoot forward (no spread shot) making it very difficult to hit anything and turning the game into more like dodging practice than anything else. And as you only get one life you will get pretty annoyed and fed up with it very quickly indeed. Do yourself a favour and never play this game. sunteam_paul Turbo Views Review
comment_zh: Pack In Video 並不以製作出色遊戲著稱。事實上他們以做出超爛遊戲聞名，Deep Blue 正是原因之一。糟糕到近乎傳奇，誰會想玩這遊戲兩次以上實在超出我的理解。畫面平淡、永遠捲動的背景毫無變化、音樂久了令人厭煩，玩法更是震驚。敵人在畫面上以 Z 字型飛來飛去，幾乎不可能不在幾秒內被打中，而且你只能朝前射擊（沒有散射），讓命中率非常低，整個遊戲變得更像閃躲練習。由於只有一條命，你會很快地感到煩躁厭煩。做你自己一個大忙，千萬別玩這款遊戲。sunteam_paul Turbo Views Review
wiki_extract_en: Deep Blue is a 1989 horizontally scrolling shooter video game developed by Hi-Score Media Work and published by Pack-In-Video for the TurboGrafx-16. The player controls a submersible fighter shaped as a freshwater angelfish that must fight through waves of mutated marine life.

title_jp: 電脳天使 デジタルアンジュ
overview_en: Anime style digital comic. Images Manual Front Cover • Manual Back Cover • Back Cover • Back Cover (inside) • Spine • CD • Reg Card Front • Reg Card Back
overview_zh: 動畫風格的數位漫畫。圖片：說明書正面・說明書背面・背面封面・背面封面（內側）・書背・CD・登錄卡正面・登錄卡背面
no_intro_name: Dennou Tenshi - Digital Angel (Tokuma Shoten Intermedia) (Japan)

title_jp: デビルクラッシュナグザットピンボール
overview_en: Building on its predecessor, Alien Crush, Devil Crash now involves a three-screen high pinball table that scrolls (instead of flip-screen). The setting is very much gothic and demonology, with many moving targets and single screen mini-games for variety. The centre part of the table also includes the head of a woman that gradually transforms into a lizard.
overview_zh: 建構於前作 Alien Crush 之上的作品，Devil Crash 採用三螢幕高的彈珠台並會捲動（取代翻頁切換）。設定非常哥德與惡魔學風格，有許多移動靶與單螢幕小遊戲增添變化。桌面中央部位還有一顆女性頭像，會逐漸變化成蜥蜴。
comment_en: The sequel to Alien Crush manages to surpass the original in every way. The  table is  so well designed that repeated play never gets boring - there are plenty of targets to aim for, loads of little critters scooting about the place and the sub games break up the action well enough, even if they aren't overly exciting. Presentation is top notch and the soundtrack is a masterpiece. It may only be a single tune playing in the main game (as opposed to Alien Crush's choice of two), but you’ll never want to turn it off. Sound effects are excellent with some nice sampled grunts and a spooky laugh. This is one of the best, if not the best, pinball game on any console ever. sunteam_paul
comment_zh: Alien Crush 的續作在各方面都超越了原作。彈珠台設計得極為精良，反覆遊玩也不會膩——有很多可以瞄準的目標、畫面上有大量小生物四處跑動，子遊戲也能有效地打破節奏，即便不算特別刺激。整體呈現頂級，配樂是傑作。雖然主要遊戲只有一首曲子（而不像 Alien Crush 有兩首可選），但你永遠不會想把它關掉。音效極佳，有不錯的採樣聲響和詭異的笑聲。這是任何主機上彈珠遊戲中最好的幾款之一，甚至可能是最好的。sunteam_paul

overview_en: Based (very) loosely on the movie of the same name, Die Hard the game is a Commando/Bloody Wolf style run-along-and-shoot affair that takes you through varying stages to defeat the hostage-takers in the Nakatomi building.
overview_zh: （極為）鬆散地改編自同名電影，Die Hard 這款遊戲是《Commando》/《Bloody Wolf》風格的邊跑邊射擊類型，帶你穿越各種階段擊敗 Nakatomi 大樓的挾持者。
comment_en: This game  has very little to do with the movie. All you do is trudge around the bland levels shooting aimlessly at the bad guys, wishing that one of them would put you out of your misery. It’s repetitive and very boring. And why the hell do you have to work your way through a forest and a river? Where was that in the movie? Did I miss something? The graphics are unimaginative and manage to be purely functional rather than attractive, and at not point do you feel any link to the source material, as though the game was almost finished when they landed the licence. Steer well clear of this one. sunteam_paul
comment_zh: 這款遊戲跟電影幾乎沒什麼關聯。你只是在平淡的關卡中跑來跑去，漫無目的地對壞蛋開槍，巴不得有人能把你從這痛苦中解救出來。重複又無聊。而且為什麼你得穿越森林和河流？電影裡有這段嗎？是我漏看了什麼嗎？畫面毫無想像力，純粹實用導向而非美觀，也完全感受不到跟原作的連結，彷彿遊戲都快做完了才拿到授權。這款遠遠避開就對了。sunteam_paul
wiki_extract_en: Die Hard is the name of four video games, one released for MS-DOS systems in 1989, one released for the Commodore 64 in 1990, one released for the PC Engine in 1990 and the other for the NES in 1991 by Activision. Its gameplay is based on the 1988 film of the same name. During the game, the player rescues hostages and battles with terrorists from a top view perspective at Nakatomi Plaza in Los Angeles.

title_jp: デジタルチャンプ バトルボクシング
overview_en: Take to the ring and fight against strangely familiar looking opponents in this first-person boxing game. Block and dodge with the D-Pad while using the buttons to punch with either left or right fists. Hold the button down and you get a more powerful hit, but it requires more timing and accuracy to be effective.
overview_zh: 登上擂台，在這款第一人稱拳擊遊戲中對抗長相莫名眼熟的對手。用方向鍵格擋與閃避，用按鍵以左拳或右拳出擊。按住按鍵可以打出更強的攻擊，但需要更精準的時機和準度才能奏效。
comment_en: As boxing games go, this isn’t initially that bad. It looks good and the controls work well enough but it’s just too limited for prolonged play. Each match goes on for way too long, and you will probably turn it off out of boredom before you even finish a complete game. It's a pity as with some refinement it could have been a fun game, but as it stands I can't justify it at all. sunteam_paul
comment_zh: 就拳擊遊戲來說，這款一開始並不算太差。畫面不錯，操作也算順手，但長期遊玩就顯得太受限了。每場比賽拖得太久，你大概在完成一整局前就會因為無聊而關機。很可惜，多做點調整這會是款有趣的遊戲，但就目前狀態我實在找不到推薦理由。sunteam_paul

overview_en: Unreleased horizontal shoot 'em up. Images Preview from PC Engine Fan June 1993
overview_zh: 未發行的橫向捲動射擊遊戲。圖片：1993 年 6 月 PC Engine Fan 雜誌預告

overview_en: Romantic life simulation with a school theme. Images Front Cover • Back Cover • Spine • CD • Reg Card Front • Reg Card Back • Addendum
overview_zh: 以校園為主題的戀愛生活模擬遊戲。圖片：封面、封底、書背、CD、登錄卡正面、登錄卡背面、補充說明。
wiki_extract_en:
Dōkyūsei (同級生; lit. Classmates) is an erotic dating sim visual novel. Originally released in 1992 by ELF Corporation for the PC-98, it became a foundational title in the dating sim genre and spawned a video game series.
A heavily altered 1999 Windows port was the basis of a 2021 remake, which received English and Chinese releases in 2022. A Game Boy Color mahjong game with Dōkyūsei characters, Jankyusei (雀級生), was released in 2001.

overview_en: Very much in the mould of Bubble Bobble, Don Doko Don features a couple of what look like garden gnomes wielding hammers to club their enemies into submission. Once you stun a baddie, walk into them to pick them up. You can then toss them across the screen (into other baddies to dispatch them also) to destroy them and create some bonus fruit or sweeties. Clear a screen of the allocated number and it's on to the next one, leading up to a boss at the end of the section. Turbo Views Review
overview_zh: 風格相當接近泡泡龍（Bubble Bobble），Don Doko Don裡登場的是一對看起來像園藝小矮人的角色，揮舞鐵鎚把敵人打到屈服。把壞蛋打暈之後，走過去就能把他們撿起來。接著可以把他們扔向螢幕另一端（砸到其他壞蛋還能一併解決），打爆他們同時掉出獎勵水果或糖果。清掉畫面上指定數量的敵人就能進到下一關，每個區段結尾還會有頭目戰。Turbo Views評測。
wiki_extract_en:
Don Doko Don is a platform arcade game developed and released by Taito in 1989. In the game, the player(s) control two lumberjacks, Bob and Jim, with the objective being to clear the screen of all the enemies. Bob and Jim use their mallets to stun the enemies, pick up the enemies, then throw them at a wall, or other enemies to kill them off, resulting in bonus points. Bonus items also appear during stages that will have varying effects on the players.
Taito and ITL ported the game to both the Family Computer and PC-Engine in 1990, both of which remained exclusive to Japan. A sequel game, aptly named Don Doko Don 2, was released in 1992 for the Family Computer in Japan, developed by Natsume Co., Ltd. The gameplay has been often compared to Taito's earlier platform games, specifically Bubble Bobble and The NewZealand Story. The original arcade version of the game was included in both Taito Memories Joukan and Taito Legends 2 in 2005, the Egret II Mini in 2022 and the Arcade Archives series in 2023.

title_jp: ドラえもん迷宮大作戦
overview_en: Based on the Kids anime about a robot cat from the future, Doraemon is a sort of Bomber Man in reverse. Instead of planting bombs, you dig holes in the ground to trap enemies then fill them in to finish them off. The game has a couple of difficulty settings, the harder providing more moving enemies to avoid or trap. There is a selection of different levels that alternate for variety (there are 60 levels in total), and the occasional boss thrown in for good measure. Turbo Views Review
overview_zh: 改編自大家熟悉的兒童動畫，描述一隻來自未來的機器貓，《哆啦A夢》玩起來有點像反過來的炸彈超人（Bomber Man）。不是放炸彈，而是在地上挖洞把敵人困住，再把洞填回去收拾他們。遊戲有幾種難度設定，較難的模式會有更多會移動的敵人要閃避或困住。關卡種類也有好幾種，交替登場增加變化（總共60關），偶爾還會安排頭目戰增添風味。Turbo Views評測。

title_jp: ドラえもん のび太のドラビアンナイト
overview_en: Taking a different route to its maze-based predecessor, this Doraemon game is a side scrolling platformer with a simple, colourful look. You have to rescue your friends who have dived into various books and are pursued by the inhabitants within. You start off having to avoid the wandering beasties, but eventually get hold of such items as a gun that freezes anything in your path for a moment. Along the way you get to ride on dinosaurs, crawl under moving stone blocks, creep along precarious ledges and slide down water chutes.
overview_zh: 和前作的迷宮路線不同，這款哆啦A夢是橫向捲動的平台動作遊戲，畫面簡潔色彩鮮豔。你必須拯救那些跳進各種書本、被書中居民追殺的朋友。一開始只能閃躲到處遊蕩的怪獸，但之後可以取得各種道具，例如能把擋路物短暫冰凍的槍。途中還可以騎恐龍、從移動石塊下面爬過去、沿著險峻的窄道前進、或是滑下水道。
wiki_extract_en: Doraemon: Nobita's Dinosaur (ドラえもん: のび太の恐竜, Doraemon: Nobita no Kyōryū) is a 1980 Japanese animated science fiction adventure film based on the manga series Doraemon, particularly the first volume of the same name of the Doraemon Long Stories series. The film premiered on March 15, 1980 in Japan. It is the first feature-length Doraemon film. In 2006, the film received a remake called Doraemon: Nobita's Dinosaur 2006 as the first feature-length film for the 2005 reboot. It is an expanded version of the 1975 chapter of the same name. It became the highest-grossing animated film of 1980.

title_jp: 双載龍 DOUBLE DRAGON II The Revenge
overview_en: Conversion of the popular arcade scrolly beat 'em up. Turbo Views Review
overview_zh: 人氣大型電玩橫向捲動格鬥動作遊戲的移植版。Turbo Views評測。
wiki_extract_en:
Double Dragon II: The Revenge is a 1988 beat 'em up video game developed and published by Technōs Japan for arcades. It is the first sequel to Double Dragon, released during the previous year. The sequel involves Billy and Jimmy Lee in a mission to avenge Billy's girlfriend Marian after she is shot to death by the Black Warriors leader Willy, who is retaliating against the Lee brothers after his defeat at the end of the previous game. 
Double Dragon II was initially developed as an upgrade kit for the original Double Dragon, but evolved into a stand-alone game due to an increase in memory size, resulting in the developers reusing assets for both games. A distinct version of the same name was released for the Nintendo Entertainment System.

overview_en: 3D Dungeon Master style romp where you navigate around complex mazes, fighting various fantasy monsters while trying to reach the exit.
overview_zh: 3D《Dungeon Master》式的冒險遊戲，你要在複雜的迷宮中穿梭，和各種奇幻怪獸戰鬥，同時設法抵達出口。

overview_en: Fast cyberpunk shoot ‘em up set in 2099 where you ride your jet-bike through waves of mecha-based bad guys.
overview_zh: 設定在2099年的快節奏賽博龐克射擊遊戲，你騎著噴射摩托車穿越一波又一波的機甲敵人。
wiki_extract_en: Down Load is a 1990 horizontally scrolling shooter published by NEC Avenue for the PC Engine. It was followed by Download 2 and also inspired an anime OVA.

overview_en: Shoot 'em up sequel that takes place over a series of very different and sometimes surreal landscapes.
overview_zh: 射擊遊戲續作，關卡橫跨一系列風格迥異、有時相當超現實的場景。

title_jp: ダウンタウン熱血物語
overview_en: Cute scrolling action game.
overview_zh: 可愛風格的捲動動作遊戲。

title_jp: ダウンタウン熱血行進曲それゆけ大運動会
overview_en: Cute scrolling beat-and-race 'em up.
overview_zh: 可愛風格的捲動格鬥兼競速遊戲。

title_jp: ドラゴンボールZ偉大なる孫悟空伝説
overview_en: One-on-one beat 'em up based on the massively popular anime. Kick, punch, shoot energy balls and fly while combating one of six characters from the series.
overview_zh: 改編自超人氣動畫的一對一格鬥遊戲。踢擊、拳擊、發射氣功波、飛行，與原作中六位角色之一一較高下。
no_intro_name: Dragon Ball Z - Idainaru Son Gokuu Densetsu (Japan) (FABT)

overview_en: A girl and her egg, all alone in the cruel world. But this is not just an ordinary egg, collect or buy  power-ups (from handy local stores) and out pops a little dragon's head, ready to swing round and breathe fire at anything that dares to get in your way. Collect enough of them and the dragon hatches fully, allowing you to ride on its back, spraying enemies with fire balls. All this is done over scenery ranging from woodlands to strangely un-fantasy like hi-tech fortresses.
overview_zh: 一個女孩和她的蛋，孤伶伶地活在殘酷的世界裡。但這可不是普通的蛋，收集或在方便的當地商店購買強化道具，蛋就會冒出一顆小小的龍頭，隨時能轉向噴火燒掉擋路的傢伙。收集到足夠數量後，龍會完全孵化，讓你騎在牠背上，朝敵人噴灑火球。整段旅程橫跨各種場景，從森林到看起來完全不奇幻、反而挺高科技的堡壘都有。

overview_en: Based on the crazy manga/anime, Dragon Half is a board game style of affair, similar to 21 Emon or some of the Peachboy titles. You progress around a board by rolling a dice, competing against other human or computer controlled players. Landing on different coloured squares has different effects. Some of these cause special events, others trigger animated battle sequences that require precisely timed button pushes for maximum impact.
overview_zh: 改編自風格瘋狂的漫畫／動畫《Dragon Half》，本作是類似《21 Emon》或某些《桃太郎》系列的桌上遊戲風格。透過擲骰子在棋盤上前進，與其他真人或電腦玩家競爭。踩到不同顏色的格子會觸發不同效果，有些會發動特殊事件，有些則會觸發動畫戰鬥畫面，需要精準抓準時機按鍵才能打出最大威力。
comment_en: While fans will find this fun and funny for a short time, the reliance on Japanese to enjoy it severely hampers the gameplay experience and as such, this game remains unrated. sunteam_paul
comment_zh: 粉絲短時間內會覺得有趣又好笑，但整個遊戲體驗嚴重依賴日文，大大影響了遊玩樂趣，因此本作不予評分。sunteam_paul

title_jp: ドラゴンナイト アンド グラフィティー
overview_en: Fantasy role-playing game with hentai overtones.
overview_zh: 帶有工口元素的奇幻角色扮演遊戲。
wiki_extract_en: Dragon Knight is a fantasy-themed eroge role-playing video game and the original entry in the Dragon Knight franchise originally developed and published by ELF Corporation in 1989. Its remake was created by NEC Avenue in 1995. Both versions were released only in Japan. The game was adapted into an OVA in 1991 and was followed by Dragon Knight II in 1990.

overview_en: Fantasy role-playing game famous for its plethora of  semi-naked girls throughout.
overview_zh: 以從頭到尾一堆半裸少女而聞名的奇幻角色扮演遊戲。
wiki_extract_en: Dragon Knight is a fantasy-themed eroge role-playing video game and the original entry in the Dragon Knight franchise originally developed and published by ELF Corporation in 1989. Its remake was created by NEC Avenue in 1995. Both versions were released only in Japan. The game was adapted into an OVA in 1991 and was followed by Dragon Knight II in 1990.

overview_en: Hentai fantasy role-playing game.
overview_zh: 工口奇幻角色扮演遊戲。
wiki_extract_en: Dragon Knight (ドラゴンナイト, Doragon Naito) is an eroge/role-playing video game series by the game company ELF. There are four Dragon Knight games released between 1989 and 1997. There is also a hentai OVA series based on it, as well as some other media including audio CDs, novels and comic books. Dragon Knight is set in a sword and sorcery setting and mostly tell the story of Yamato Takeru (no relation with the historical figure of Yamato Takeru), a wayward young swordsman dedicated to saving damsels and fighting evil.

overview_en: The sequel to Dragon Spirit follows much in the same vein, with your flying dragon defeating prehistoric monsters over various landscapes. This time round there is a two player mode thrown in for extra appeal.
overview_zh: Dragon Spirit的續作延續了大致相同的風格，操控飛龍擊敗各種場景中的史前怪獸。這次還加入了雙人模式，讓遊戲更有吸引力。
wiki_extract_en: Dragon Saber is a 1990 vertically scrolling shooter video game developed and published by Namco for arcades. It was released in Japan in December 1990 and Europe in 1991. It is a sequel to Dragon Spirit.

title_jp: ドラゴンスレイヤー英雄伝説
overview_en: An overhead, random battle, fantasy role-playing game, Dragon Slayer - The Legend of Heroes comes from Falcom, the company behind the Ys series.  All the genre staples are here: a group of up to four characters, weapon and item shops...you know the drill by now. Battles are random and turn based, with the option of using either CD music or PSG which avoids that little extra CD access time (this also applies to village tunes). Later in the game, you are able to see the creatures moving about the landscape, making avoiding battles that much easier if you are just trying to get from A to B without hassle. The plot is hardly anything original, involving the player taking the role of the young prince escaping from his evil guardian and adventuring into the dangerous world outside. The storyline does however develop into something much wider and more dramatic as the game progresses, and is split into several chapters to help mark your progress. Comment (US Version) This is another brilliant Falcom RPG. I am not a great fan of the random battle style of gameplay, but Falcom has removed a lot of the frustration by allowing you to see your foes as you progress. Added to this the speed of the battles and the great music that accompanies them and they feel far less tiresome than many other games of this type. The plot is simple at first, but compelling enough to keep you playing, and a lot of enjoyment comes from the little quirks that some of the townspeople exhibit. It really is worth talking to everyone as you get some amusing responses. Graphically the game is more functional than beautiful, but sonically it soars with a superb soundtrack that, while it doesn't quite compare with Ys, still has its high moments. The arrangement is perhaps less that I would have expected, particularly compared to the music on the Perfect Collection, but very worthy nonetheless. The PSG music has some fantastic sampled drums too, some of the best on the Engine. Overall, Dragon Slayer is my favourite turn-based-battle RPG on the PC Engine, it's a lengthy and enjoyable quest, with a decent English language translation which is only let down by some of the worst voice acting you will ever hear. But ignore that and buy it. sunteam_paul Turbo Views Review
overview_zh: 俯視視角、隨機遇敵的奇幻角色扮演遊戲，《Dragon Slayer - The Legend of Heroes》由推出Ys系列的Falcom開發。該類型的基本要素這裡都有：最多四人的角色隊伍、武器店、道具店……你懂的。戰鬥採隨機遇敵、回合制，可選擇CD音樂或PSG音源，後者可避開CD讀取的額外等待時間（村莊音樂也同樣適用）。遊戲後期可以在地圖上看到怪物移動，若只是想從A點移動到B點不想被騷擾，要避開戰鬥就輕鬆多了。劇情說不上有什麼原創性，玩家扮演一位年輕王子，從邪惡監護人手中逃脫，踏入外面危險的世界展開冒險。不過故事隨著遊戲進展會發展得更宏大、更戲劇化，並分成數個章節幫助玩家掌握進度。評語（美版）這又是一款優秀的Falcom角色扮演遊戲。我其實不太喜歡隨機遇敵的戰鬥方式，但Falcom讓你能看到敵人、提前反應，大幅減少了挫折感。再加上戰鬥節奏明快、搭配優秀的音樂，比起許多同類型遊戲實在少了很多疲倦感。劇情起初很簡單，但足夠引人入勝讓你想一直玩下去，和城鎮NPC互動時會看到一些古怪有趣的反應，讓人很想到處跟人講話，會得到不少好笑的回應。畫面方面機能性大於美感，但音樂表現非常出色，雖然和Ys還不能完全相比，但仍有不少精彩段落。編曲或許不如我原本期待，特別是和Perfect Collection專輯的音樂相比之下，但依然相當出色。PSG音樂裡有幾段非常棒的取樣鼓點，是PC Engine上最出色的之一。整體來說，Dragon Slayer是我在PC Engine上最愛的回合制戰鬥RPG，劇情冗長但樂趣十足，附有不錯的英文翻譯，唯一拖後腿的是史上最糟糕的配音之一。但別管配音，買就對了。sunteam_paul。Turbo Views評測。

title_en: DRAGON SLAYER - THE LEGEND OF HEROES II
title_jp: ドラゴンスレイヤー英雄伝説II
overview_en: Similar looking sequel to the popular original, set in the same area but with a new generation of characters.
overview_zh: 外觀相似的人氣原作續作，場景設定在同一區域，但登場的是新世代的角色。
no_intro_name: Dragon Slayer - The Legend of Heroes II (Japan)
wiki_extract_en:
Dragon Slayer: The Legend of Heroes is a 1989 role-playing video game developed by Nihon Falcom. It is the sixth game in the Dragon Slayer series and the first in The Legend of Heroes franchise.
It was originally released in 1989 for the NEC PC-8801. Within the next few years, it would also be ported to the NEC PC-9801, MSX2, PC Engine CD-ROM, X68000, Mega Drive, and Super Famicom. A Dragon Slayer: The Legend of Heroes Barcode Battler card set was also released by Epoch Co. in 1992. The PC Engine version was released in the United States for the TurboGrafx-CD, and was the only game in the series released in the US until The Legend of Heroes: A Tear of Vermillion, the PlayStation Portable remake.
In 1995, a version of the game was broadcast exclusively for Japanese markets via the Super Famicom's Satellaview subunit under the name BS Dragon Slayer Eiyu Densetsu. In 1998, a remake of The Legend of Heroes was bundled with a remake of Dragon Slayer: The Legend of Heroes II and was released for both the PlayStation and Sega Saturn.
wiki_title: Dragon Slayer: The Legend of Heroes

overview_en: This vertically scrolling shoot 'em up uses the original idea of casting the player as a dragon flying over prehistoric landscapes. You can fire both forwards and drop bombs to destroy ground based enemies, and  collect power-ups that increase your firepower and also increase the number of heads you have! Turbo Views Review
overview_zh: 這款縱向捲動射擊遊戲採用了一個原創的點子：讓玩家扮演一隻飛越史前地景的巨龍。可以向前射擊、也可以投下炸彈摧毀地面敵人，還能收集強化道具，增加火力的同時也會增加你的龍頭數量！Turbo Views評測。
wiki_extract_en:
Dragon Spirit is a 1987 vertically scrolling shooter video game developed and published by Namco for arcades. It was released by Atari Games in North America. Controlling the dragon Amur, the player must complete each of the game's nine areas to rescue the princess Alicia from the demon Zawell. Similar to Namco's own Xevious, Amur has a projectile weapon for destroying air-based enemies and a bomb for destroying ground enemies. It ran on the Namco System 1 arcade board.
Music for the game was composed by Shinji Hosoe and designed by Tatsuya Ishikawa. Hosoe, previously a graphics artist for Namco, was allowed to work on the music himself due to most of the company's composers working on Genpei Tōma Den. However, much of his work was accidentally wiped from the hardware midway through development, forcing him to start over. Ishikawa previously created character artwork for Rolling Thunder, being assigned to the project after taking an interest in the enemy designs. The game was dedicated to Shouichi Fukatani, a Namco programmer who had died a few years earlier in 1985 and worked on many of the company's older games, such as Dig Dug and Super Pac-Man.
The game was ported to several home systems, including the X68000, TurboGrafx-16 and Atari ST, and is included in various Namco compilations. The arcade version of Dragon Spirit was met with positive reviews from critics for standing out amongst other shooter games, being praised for its Paleozoic setting, graphics and soundtrack. Home versions were met with a more mixed reception, being criticized for its high difficulty and inferior graphics and music. It was followed by two sequels, Dragon Spirit: The New Legend (1988) and Dragon Saber (1990). A ShiftyLook webcomic adaptation of the game was also produced.

title_jp: ドロップロックほらホラ
overview_en: A bizarre sort of Breakout/Arkanoid clone, you are presented with a constantly dropping wall of various items (apples, melons...) and have to destroy as many as possible by rebounding the small bouncing ball around the screen. Pushing the button toggles between 'open' and 'closed', which affects whether the ball will bounce in a shallow or steep angle when it hits. Missing the ball will eventually destroy the barrier behind you so you must keep your wits about you at all times. Turbo Views Review
overview_zh: 一款有點奇怪的打磚塊（Breakout／Arkanoid）變體遊戲，畫面上會有一整牆不斷掉落的各種物品（蘋果、西瓜……），你必須把小小的彈球打來打去，盡可能破壞更多物品。按鈕可以在「開」和「關」之間切換，影響球撞上時的反彈角度是淺還是陡。漏接球的話，你身後的屏障會逐漸被破壞，所以必須隨時保持警覺。Turbo Views評測。

title_jp: ダンジョン エクスプローラー
overview_en: At first, Dungeon Explorer seems like a straightforward Gauntlet clone, but instead of a repeated set of non-stop levels, it is planned as more of an adventure role-playing game with villages to visit and a storyline running through the game. There are several characters to choose from at the beginning which is more than just a cosmetic choice as they do vary in their stats. While exploring, the Gauntlet similarities become apparent with destroyable generators that spit out monsters ad infinitum, but also other traps and puzzles like fire spitting gargoyles and pushable rocks. There are two types of special potions to pick up, the results varying on the character you are playing, but typical effects are smart bomb style damage, or armour increase. Dungeons end with a boss that drops a coloured crystal that is used to upgrade your character.
overview_zh: 乍看之下，Dungeon Explorer像是單純的Gauntlet模仿作，但它不是一關接一關的重複關卡，而是規劃得更像角色扮演冒險，有村莊可以走訪、也有貫穿全作的劇情。開頭可以選擇多個角色，不只是外觀差異，數值也各有不同。在探索過程中，和Gauntlet的相似之處就浮現出來了——有可破壞的怪物產生器不斷吐出怪物，還有會噴火的石像鬼、可推動的石頭等陷阱與謎題。可收集兩種特殊藥水，效果依角色而異，但常見效果包括全螢幕攻擊或提升防禦力。地下城結尾都會有頭目，擊敗後會掉落用來強化角色的彩色水晶。
comment_en: Dungeon Explorer is one of the classic PC Engine HuCARDs. Although it's an action RPG, the role playing element is fairly light and is there to add some flavour to the game between dungeons - if you're playing the Japanese version you'll still have a great time. Animation is of a good quality and the look of the game is dark and atmospheric, but where it really stands tall is the music which is one of the finest chip generated soundtracks on the machine. It's a lot of fun and additcive to play and serves as a great introduction to its vastly expanded and superior sequel. sunteam_paul
comment_zh: Dungeon Explorer是PC Engine HuCard卡匣中的經典之一。雖然是動作RPG，但角色扮演元素其實不多，主要用來在地下城之間增添一點風味，即便玩日版也能玩得很盡興。動畫品質不錯，畫面風格黑暗有氣氛，但真正出彩的是音樂，是這台主機上最優秀的晶片音源配樂之一。玩起來樂趣十足、令人上癮，也是進入內容更豐富、品質更上乘的續作前的絕佳入門。sunteam_paul
wiki_extract_en:
Dungeon Explorer is an action role-playing video game developed by Atlus for the TurboGrafx-16 and originally published by Hudson Soft in Japan on March 4, 1989, and later in North America by NEC on November 15 of the same year. The first installment in the eponymous franchise, the game is set in the land of Oddesia, which has been overrun by an alien race and where players assume the role of one of eight main characters tasked with recovering the Ora stone to kill the alien king Natas. Co-directed by Kazutoshi Ueda and Yōsuke Niino, the title was created by most of the same team that would work on later several projects such as entries in the Megami Tensei series. Though it was initially launched for the TurboGrafx-16, it was later re-released through download services for various consoles.
Dungeon Explorer garnered positive reception from critics during its initial release and is considered a pioneer title in the action role-playing game genre due to its co-operative multiplayer gameplay for up to five players, but has been met with a more mixed response from reviewers in recent years. It was followed by four sequels: Dungeon Explorer II (1993), Dungeon Explorer for Sega CD and Crystal Beans from Dungeon Explorer (1995), as well as Dungeon Explorer: Warriors of Ancient Arts (2007).

title_jp: ダンジョンエクスプローラーII
overview_en: This CD sequel to Dungeon Explorer plays exactly the same as the first game. You chose your character from a variety of fantasy stereotypes and set forth in a Gauntlet-esque adventure. The CD format allows a greater quantity and variety of levels to compliment the storyline, with new areas and characters becoming open to you as you progress. Turbo Views Review
overview_zh: Dungeon Explorer的CD續作，玩法與前作一模一樣。從多種奇幻典型角色中挑選後踏上一段Gauntlet風格的冒險。CD格式帶來了更多數量與種類的關卡來搭配劇情，隨著進度推進會開啟新區域與新角色。Turbo Views評測。
wiki_extract_en:
Dungeon Explorer II is an action role-playing video game developed and originally published by Hudson Soft for the TurboDuo in Japan on March 26, 1993, and in North America by Turbo Technologies in October of the same year. A sequel to 1989's Dungeon Explorer, it is the second installment in the eponymous franchise.
Set a century after the fall of alien king Natas in the original Dungeon Explorer, players assume the role of one of the eight main characters tasked with recovering the Ora stone from the evil magician Fades, who stole it from king Earl II to resurrect Natas in order to save the land of Oddesia from destruction while also rescuing princess Miriam of Solis, who was taken as hostage by a monster. Dungeon Explorer II was later re-released through download services for various consoles.
Dungeon Explorer II was met with mostly positive reception from critics and reviewers since its initial release, with praise directed towards the graphics, audio, gameplay and multiplayer support.

title_jp: ダンジジョンマスターセエロンズ・クエスト
maker: VICTOR MUSICAL INDUSTRIES / SOFTWARE HEAVEN / FTL GAMES
overview_en: 3D dungeon crawler.
overview_zh: 3D迷宮探索遊戲。
wiki_extract_en:
Dungeon Master is a role-playing video game featuring a pseudo-3D first-person perspective. It was developed and published by FTL Games for the Atari ST in 1987, almost identical Amiga and PC (DOS) ports following in 1988 and 1992.
Dungeon Master sold 40,000 copies in its year of release alone, and went on to become the ST's best-selling game of all time. The game was an influential early example of the first-person party-based dungeon crawler genre, with later examples including Eye of the Beholder (1991).

title_jp: 超英雄伝説ダイナスティックヒーロー
overview_en: A conversion of the Sega Mega Drive Wonderboy IV, Dynastic Hero is more akin to Adventure Island (Master System Wonderboy III) and Bikkuri Man World (Arcade Wonderboy II) than the other Wonderboy games. It plays very much as a platform RPG, with the ability to buy weapons and armour, upgrading from swords to spears and so forth. Exploration is not as deep as a proper RPG though, it's pretty obvious where to head next and each location is basically a 'level' in itself, complete with a typical array of Wonderboy-esque monsters who drop coins as you kill them. Gameplay is pretty easy to pick up: just run and stab, duck when you need to and perform a special move (such as a spear-twirl) by holding up on the pad as you attack. There are also some minor puzzles you encounter, such as having to play certain tunes on an ocarina to open doors. Over the course of your adventure you also gain magical powers, both offensive and defensive that can be placed into one of your two items slots for easy access while playing.
overview_zh: Mega Drive版《Wonderboy IV》的移植版，Dynastic Hero風格更接近《Adventure Island》（Master System版Wonderboy III）和《Bikkuri Man World》（大型電玩版Wonderboy II），而非其他Wonderboy系列作。玩起來非常像平台RPG，可以購買武器與護甲，從劍升級到長槍等等。探索深度沒有正統RPG那麼深，下一步該往哪走其實相當明顯，每個地點基本上就是一「關」，裡頭有典型的Wonderboy系列怪物，擊殺後會掉金幣。玩法很容易上手：跑過去戳下去、必要時蹲下、按住上鍵攻擊可使出特殊招式（例如旋轉長槍）。途中也會遇到一些小謎題，例如用陶笛吹奏特定旋律來開門。冒險過程中還能獲得攻擊與防禦的魔法，可裝備在兩個道具欄位之一，遊戲中能方便取用。

overview_en: Spooky adventure game set in Newcam, near Boston, that has the player searching through the haunted Weathertop Mansion.
overview_zh: 恐怖冒險遊戲，背景設定在波士頓附近的Newcam，玩家要在鬧鬼的Weathertop豪宅中搜查。
wiki_extract_en: Laplace no Ma is a horror-themed role-playing video game released in 1987 by HummingBirdSoft for the NEC PC-8801 and NEC PC-9801. The game was also ported to various Japanese computers and later to consoles with Super Famicom and PC Engine Super CD-ROM². It became the first part of the Gōsuto Hantā series which includes both Kurokishi no Kamen (1994), Paracelsus no Maken (1994), and Gōsuto Hantā Arukeringa no Ma Umi (2020).

overview_en: 3D adventure game based on Dungeons & Dragons. Turbo Views Review
overview_zh: 改編自龍與地下城（Dungeons & Dragons）的3D冒險遊戲。Turbo Views評測。
no_intro_name: Dungeons _ Dragons - Order of the Griffon (USA)
wiki_extract_en: Order of the Griffon is a tactical role-playing game for the TurboGrafx-16 developed by Westwood Associates and based on the tabletop role-playing game Dungeons & Dragons (D&D). The game is set in the nation of Karameikos within D&D's Mystara setting.
wiki_title: Dungeons & Dragons: Order of the Griffon

overview_en: Girly pop idol life simulation game, similar in style to Graduation.
overview_zh: 少女偶像生活模擬遊戲，風格類似《Graduation》（卒業）。

overview_en: Arcade conversion of Capcom's Chinese themed mounted scrolling beat 'em up.
overview_zh: Capcom中華風格騎馬捲動動作遊戲的大型電玩移植版。
wiki_extract_en:
Tenchi wo Kurau II may refer to:

Tenchi wo Kurau II: Shokatsu Kōmei Den, a 1991 role-playing video game
Tenchi wo Kurau 2: Sekiheki no Tatakai, a 1992 beat-'em-up video game released outside Japan as Warriors of Fate

title_jp: ザ・デビスカップテニス
overview_en: Split screen tennis game with several play modes including exhibition, doubles, tournament, practice and the Davis Cup itself. Turbo Views Review
overview_zh: 分割畫面網球遊戲，內含多種模式，包括表演賽、雙打、錦標賽、練習，以及台維斯盃本身。Turbo Views評測。

title_jp: エフェラアンドジリオラジ・エンブレムフロムダークネス
overview_en: Mostly overhead action RPG featuring a couple of girlies that 2 players can play simultaneously.
overview_zh: 以俯視視角為主的動作RPG，主角是一對女孩，支援兩人同時遊玩。

title_jp: 栄冠は君に 高校野球全国大会
overview_en: In-depth baseball management sinulation. Virtually incomprehensible if you cannot read Japanese. Mouse compatible.
overview_zh: 深度棒球球隊經營模擬遊戲。不懂日文幾乎玩不下去。支援滑鼠。
no_intro_name: Eikan wa Kimi ni - Koukou Yakyuu Zenkoku Taikai (Artdink) (Japan)

overview_en: Oriental strategy game.
overview_zh: 東方風格策略遊戲。

maker: NEC HOME ELECTRONICS / MEDIA WORKS / GLODIA / RIGHT STUFF
overview_en: Overhead RPG featuring a transforming dragon.
overview_zh: 俯視視角RPG，主角是一隻會變身的龍。
wiki_extract_en: Emerald Dragon (エメラルドドラゴン, Emerarudo Doragon) is a role-playing video game developed by Glodia and released for multiple platforms in Japan. It was first launched for NEC Corporation's PC-8801 and PC-9801 home computers on December 22, 1989, followed by conversions for the X68000 (released on December 6, 1990), MSX2 (released on December 26 of the same year), and FM Towns (released on May 28, 1992). Developer Alfa System later produced console versions of the game for the PC Engine in Super CD-ROM² format (released on January 29, 1994) and the Super Famicom (released on July 28, 1995). The game features characters and locations based on Zoroastrian mythology.

overview_en: An action adventure with push-screen scrolling, you must run, jump, climb and shoot your way through a destroyed city and caves fighting mutants.
overview_zh: 推進式畫面捲動的動作冒險遊戲，你必須一路跑跳、攀爬、射擊，穿越毀滅的城市與洞窟，對抗變種生物。

title_jp: 都市転送計画エターナルシティ
overview_en: Horizontal run and shoot game with some mecha design by the legendary Masamune Shirow.
overview_zh: 橫向跑動射擊遊戲，部分機甲設計出自傳奇漫畫家士郎正宗之手。

title_jp: エグザイル　時の狭間に
overview_en: Action RPG that uses an overhead viewpoint for story-telling 'town' areas, but switches to a side-on view similar to Cadash to provide the thrills.
overview_zh: 動作RPG，「城鎮」劇情部分採俯視視角，動作場景則切換為類似《Cadash》的橫向視角帶來刺激感。
comment_en: Exile is a pretty good game. The main character acts pretty cool and a little harsh at times but is quite appealing because of it, and the story has some nice ideas, but is maybe a little muddled in places. The action scenes play like an average platform game, but look nice enough so the game gets away with it. Good enough to satisfy the role-playing fever for a while. sunteam_paul Turbo Views Review �
comment_zh: Exile是一款相當不錯的遊戲。主角個性冷酷、有時甚至有點辛辣，卻也因此頗具魅力，故事也有不錯的點子，只是某些地方有點雜亂。動作場景玩起來像一般的平台遊戲，但看起來夠漂亮，所以還算說得過去。足以滿足一陣子想玩RPG的癮。sunteam_paul。Turbo Views評測。
wiki_extract_en:
Exile (エグザイル, Eguzairu; originally stylized XZR) is an action role-playing video game series developed by Telenet Japan. The first two games in the series, XZR and XZR II were both released in Japan in 1988, with versions available for the NEC PC-8801, NEC PC-9801, MSX2 and the X1 turbo (for the first game only). In 1991, a remake of XZR II simply titled Exile was released for the PC Engine and Mega Drive. These versions were both released in North America the following year, with Working Designs handling the localization for the TurboGrafx-CD version, while Renovation Products published the Genesis version. A sequel exclusive to the Super CD-ROM2 format, titled Exile: Wicked Phenomenon, was released in 1992, which was also localized by Working Designs for the North American market.
The Exile series centers on Sadler, a Syrian Assassin, who is the main character of each game. The original computer versions were notorious for featuring various references to religious historical figures, modern political leaders, iconography, drugs, and time-traveling assassins, although some of these aspects were considerably toned down or omitted in the later console games, with the English versions rewriting all the historical religious organizations into fictional groups.

title_jp: エグザイルⅡ邪念の事象
overview_en: Platform ARPG sequel that follows in a very similar vein to the original. Turbo Views Review
overview_zh: 平台動作RPG續作，風格延續原作。Turbo Views評測。

overview_en: Cute themed overhead racing game that takes you through the career of a wannabe F1 driver.
overview_zh: 可愛風格的俯視賽車遊戲，帶你經歷一位F1新秀車手的職業生涯。
wiki_extract_en: F-1 Dream (Japanese: F1ドリーム) is an automobile racing arcade game released by Capcom in 1988. Like many other Capcom arcade games at the time, it was released in the US by Romstar. It also had a PC Engine port released exclusively in Japan on August 25, 1989.

overview_en: Pole Position style Formula 1 racing game.
overview_zh: Pole Position風格的一級方程式賽車遊戲。
no_intro_name: F-1 Pilot - You're King of Kings (Japan)

overview_en: Very fast overhead Formula 1 racing game. Choose your team and race on tracks around the world.
overview_zh: 非常快節奏的俯視F1賽車遊戲。選擇你的車隊，在世界各地的賽道上競速。
wiki_extract_en: F1 Circus (エフワン サーカス, Efuwan Sākasu) is a Formula One-based racing game developed by Nihon Bussan and published by Nichibutsu for the PC Engine.

title_jp: F1サーカス'91 ワールド・チャンピオンシップ
overview_en: Racing sequel that continues the overhead high speed gameplay of its predecessor.
overview_zh: 賽車系列續作，延續前作的俯視高速玩法。
wiki_extract_en: F1 Circus (エフワン サーカス) is a series of Formula One-based racing video games developed and published by Nichibutsu starting on the PC Engine in 1990.

title_jp: F1サーカス'92ザ・スピード・オブ・サウンド
overview_en: The fast paced Forumla 1 action returns, retaining all the gameplay elements of the original.
overview_zh: 快節奏的一級方程式賽車動作再度回歸，保留原作所有玩法要素。
no_intro_name: F1 Circus '92 - The Speed of Sound (Japan)

title_jp: F1サーカス・スペシャル ポールトゥウイン
overview_en: The first CD-Rom version of the game in the series contains much the same visual and gameplay style as all the others, but adds a dictionary section and some digitized images.
overview_zh: 系列中第一款CD-ROM版本，視覺與玩法大致延續系列其他作品，但新增了辭典篇章與數位化圖像。
no_intro_name: F1 Circus Special - Pole to Win (Japan) (FAAT, FACT)
wiki_extract_en: F1 Circus (エフワン サーカス) is a series of Formula One-based racing video games developed and published by Nichibutsu starting on the PC Engine in 1990.

title_jp: F-1チームシュミレーション PROJECT F
overview_en: Formula 1 racing management simulation.
overview_zh: 一級方程式賽車車隊經營模擬遊戲。

overview_en: Split screen Formula 1 style racing game. Compete against the computer or your friends.
overview_zh: 分割畫面F1風格賽車遊戲。可對戰電腦或朋友。

overview_en: Conversion of multi-player 3D maze shooting game. Longplay Video from World of Longplays Images Front Cover • Back Cover • Spine • CD • Reg Card Front • Reg Card Back • Sample Front Cover • Sample Back Cover • Sample CD
overview_zh: 多人3D迷宮射擊遊戲的移植版。World of Longplays提供的Longplay影片。圖片：封面、封底、書背、CD、登錄卡正面、登錄卡背面、樣品封面、樣品封底、樣品CD。

overview_en: Fighter combat simulation, previously on the Commodore Amiga.
overview_zh: 戰鬥機空戰模擬遊戲，原本登場於Commodore Amiga平台。
comment_en: Some may call it a bold move to try and convert a polygon-based air combat simulation to the PC Engine, others may call it folly. But whatever your stance, what we are left with here is a slow, boring and dreadfully clunky and unenjoyable experience. Sit this alongside Afterburner II, or even the poor Battle Ace, and you'll wonder why they bothered. There is no fun to be had here. sunteam_paul Turbo Views Review
comment_zh: 有人可能會說把多邊形空戰模擬遊戲移植到PC Engine是勇敢之舉，也有人會說是蠢事一樁。但不管你怎麼看，我們拿到的結果就是又慢、又無聊、操作彆扭到令人難以忍受。把它跟Afterburner II、甚至是不怎麼樣的Battle Ace擺在一起比，你會忍不住想：他們到底為什麼要做這個？這裡完全沒有樂趣可言。sunteam_paul。Turbo Views評測。
wiki_extract_en: Falcon is a combat flight simulator video game and the first official entry (not counting the 1984's F-16 Fighting Falcon) in the Falcon series of the F-16 jet fighter's simulators by Spectrum HoloByte. Originally developed by Sphere for Macintosh and MS-DOS in 1987 and ported to several platforms between 1988 and 1992, the game earned commercial success and critical acclaim.

overview_en: This weird and colourful shoot ‘em up is quite different from any other. Instead of auto scrolling levels, they wrap around left to right, and only when you (as egg shaped Opa-Opa) have destroyed all the static ‘bases’ on each level do you progress to the boss. On top of that, there are various weapons and items that can be purchased from a floating balloon shop such as jet engines, wide shot and laser. Turbo Views Review
overview_zh: 這款怪異又繽紛的射擊遊戲相當獨特。關卡不是自動捲動，而是左右環繞循環，必須先摧毀關卡中所有靜止的「基地」，才能進入頭目戰。你操作的是蛋形角色Opa-Opa，另外還能在飄浮的氣球商店裡購買各種武器與道具，例如噴射引擎、擴散彈、雷射等等。Turbo Views評測。
wiki_extract_en:
Fantasy Zone is a 1986 horizontally scrolling shooter video game developed and published by Sega for arcades. It is the first game in the Fantasy Zone series, and was later ported to several home consoles, including Sega's Master System. The player controls a sentient spaceship named Opa-Opa who fights an enemy invasion in the titular group of planets. The game contains a number of features atypical of the traditional scrolling shooter. The main character, Opa-Opa, is sometimes referred to as Sega's first mascot character.
The game's design and main character have similarities to Konami's TwinBee, and both games are credited with establishing the cute 'em up subgenre. Fantasy Zone also popularized the concept of a boss rush, a stage where the player faces multiple previous bosses again in succession. The game was followed by several sequels, beginning with Fantasy Zone II: The Tears of Opa-Opa in 1987.

title_en: FAR EAST OF EDEN - FŪN KABUKI DEN
title_jp: 天外魔境風雲カブキ伝
overview_en: Offshoot of the Far East of Eden series, centering on the crazy character of Kabuki Danjūrō. Gameplay is much the same as Maru, and so is the menu system, which is difficult to suss out for non-Japanese speakers, particularly as it's all in Kanji.
overview_zh: Far East of Eden（天外魔境）系列的衍生作，以風格獨特的角色歌舞伎團十郎為主角。玩法大致與《卍MARU》相同，選單系統也是，對不懂日文的玩家來說相當難懂，尤其全都是漢字。
no_intro_name: Far East of Eden - Tengai Makyou - Fuun Kabuki-den (Japan)

overview_en: First of the popular turn-based RPG series set in old Japan. The following information is taken from Hudson Soft's website: "The Tengai Makyo series was conceived and produced by Ouji Hiroi. The first game in the series, Tengai Makyo Ziria, was released in 1989...as the world's first major RPG on CD-ROM. At a time when most thought CD-ROMs were a poor medium for games, Tengai Makyo Ziria integrated features that couldn't be put on other media, from large maps to animation, real-voice narration, and CD-quality music. With a score from Ryuichi Sakamoto and an all-star cast of narrators, this epoch-making title revolutionized the role-playing game. Set in the fictional Japanese world of Zipangu, the game brought adventure, romance, comedy, and drama together in a single, sprawling storyline. In addition to the main games, the series also featured fighting action games featuring characters from the RPG. The main RPG series was originally conceived in three parts...The final installment of the original series, Tengai Makyo III: Namida...was stopped due to development costs and hardware limitations..."
overview_zh: 設定在古日本、大受歡迎的回合制RPG系列第一作。以下資訊摘自Hudson Soft官方網站：「天外魔境系列由廣井王子企劃與製作。系列首作《天外魔境 ZIRIA》於1989年推出⋯⋯是世界首款大型CD-ROM RPG。當時多數人認為CD-ROM不適合做遊戲，《天外魔境 ZIRIA》卻融入了其他媒介做不到的要素，從大型地圖、動畫、真人配音旁白到CD音質音樂。搭配坂本龍一的配樂與全明星級的旁白陣容，這部劃時代作品徹底改變了角色扮演遊戲。遊戲設定在虛構的日本世界『ジパング』，將冒險、戀愛、喜劇與戲劇融入一個宏大的故事中。除本傳外，系列中也有以RPG角色為主角的格鬥動作遊戲。本傳RPG原本規劃為三部曲⋯⋯最終作《天外魔境III：Namida》⋯⋯因開發成本與硬體限制而停止開發⋯⋯」
no_intro_name: Tengai Makyou - Ziria (Hudson) (Japan)[AKA Far East of Eden - Ziria]

title_en: FAR EAST OF EDEN II - MANJI MARU
overview_en: The second in the cute-ish RPG series set in a fictional samurai era. The following information is taken from the Hudson Soft website: "King Yomi of the race of Earth, descended from the stalk of the giant Dark Orchid, was finally banished deep underground by the Fire Clan and the seven sacred swords. A thousand years later, a Tycoon with designs on Zipangu breaks the seal and releases Yomi and the Dark Orchid. It is up to the tender boy general and descendent of the Fire Clan, Sengoku Manji-maru, along with his friends, the flamboyant Kabuki Danjuro, the ferociously powerful Gokuraku Taro, and the mysterious, star-crossed Kinu, to stand up to the beasts of the race of Earth. Set in the fictional but distinctively Japanese world of Zipangu, the game's sprawling, out-of-this-world adventures track the hero of the race of Fire as he battles the great demon of the Earth....the game astoundingly sold nearly as many copies as its platform sold consoles. It has enjoyed enduring popularity, long sitting at the top of fan voting in gaming magazines. With over three thousand characters, an all-star cast of narrators including Kyoko Kishida, three hours of narration, twenty thousand map screens, animated sequences around every corner, and the full orchestra sound of Joe Hisaishi, you don't have to look hard to find the many ways in which Tengai Makyo II rewrote the book on RPGs. Each of these elements serves to develop and enhance the human dramas of the game's labyrinthine story."
overview_zh: 設定在虛構武士時代、偏可愛風格的RPG系列第二作。以下資訊摘自Hudson Soft網站：「地之一族的王者黃泉，傳承自巨大黑蘭之莖，最終被火之一族與七把聖劍封印至地底深處。一千年後，一位覬覦日本國的Tycoon破壞封印，放出了黃泉與黑蘭。身為火之一族後裔的年輕少年將領戰國卍丸，必須與華麗的歌舞伎團十郎、力量驚人的極樂太郎，以及命運坎坷、神秘的絹攜手，對抗地之一族的妖獸。故事設定在虛構卻極具日本風情的世界『ジパング』，遊戲那宏大、奇幻的冒險描繪了火之一族的英雄對抗大地之魔的過程⋯⋯本作銷量幾乎與主機出貨量相當，人氣歷久不衰，長期名列遊戲雜誌讀者票選榜首。超過三千名角色、以岸田今日子為首的全明星旁白陣容、三小時旁白、兩萬個地圖畫面、隨處可見的動畫演出，再加上久石讓的完整管弦樂配樂，處處可見《天外魔境II》如何改寫了RPG的規則。這些要素都為遊戲那錯綜複雜的故事中的人性戲劇增添了深度。」
no_intro_name: Far East of Eden - Tengai Makyou II - Manji Maru (Japan)
wiki_extract_en:
Tengai Makyō II: Manji Maru (天外魔境II卍丸, lit. "The Devil's Cave Outside Heaven: Manji Maru"), also known as Far East of Eden II: Manji Maru, is a role-playing video game and the second game in the Tengai Makyō series. It was first released in 1992 for the PC Engine Super CD-ROM² by Hudson Soft and developed by Red Company.
It was reportedly the most expensive video game developed up until that time. Upon release, it received critical acclaim and became Japan's best-selling PC Engine game of all time as of 1993. It was remade in 2003 by Hudson for the GameCube and PlayStation 2, and a version was released for the Nintendo DS in 2006. All versions of the game were released only in Japan.

title_en: FARJIUS NO JAKŌTEI - NEO METAL FANTASY
overview_en: Overhead sci-fi role-playing game. Images Front Cover • Back Cover • CD
overview_zh: 俯視視角的科幻角色扮演遊戲。圖片：封面、封底、CD。
no_intro_name: Farjius no Jakoutei - Neo Metal Fantasy (Japan) (Rev 1)

title_jp: フォーセットアムール
overview_en: Platform game with a mild Bionic Commando feel.
overview_zh: 帶有淡淡《Bionic Commando》風味的平台遊戲。

overview_en: Plot heavy platform game from the people that brought you Terraforming and Flash Hiders.
overview_zh: 劇情份量十足的平台遊戲，出自推出過《Terraforming》和《Flash Hiders》的同一班底。

overview_en: Mecha-based game where you pick your robot, choose the stats, then go head to head on a dangerous race course, beating your opponent into dust.
overview_zh: 以機甲為主題的遊戲，選擇自己的機器人、配置數值後，便在充滿危險的賽道上與對手正面對決，把對方打成碎片。

overview_en: Conversion of Capcom's pressure pad one-on-one beat 'em up that paved the way for the most influential sequel of all time. Turbo Views Review
overview_zh: Capcom壓力感應按鈕一對一格鬥遊戲的移植版，也為史上最具影響力的續作鋪了路。Turbo Views評測。

title_jp: ファイナルブラスター
overview_en: Overhead sci-fi shoot 'em up with all the usual elements.
overview_zh: 俯視視角的科幻射擊遊戲，包含所有常見的要素。

title_jp: ファイナルラップ・ツイン
overview_en: Final Lap was a Namco arcade game from 1987 and was a direct successor to their Pole Position franchise. The game was based on “scaler” design popularized by Sega’s Out Run.   The vehicle had multiple frames of animation as it turned on the track,   but the car seemed a bit floaty and the scenery lacked much of the   interesting things flying by at the side of the road. The home   version strips down the 8 networked arcade machines to a simple split   screen couch co-op.  Adding longevity to the mix is an additional quest   mode which may look and seem like an afterthought, but is actually a   reason to choose this title over other racers on the PC Engine.
overview_zh: Final Lap是Namco 1987年推出的大型電玩，為Pole Position系列的直系後作。遊戲採用了Sega《Out Run》帶動的「縮放精靈」式設計。車輛在賽道上轉彎時有多張動畫幀，但車體感覺有點飄，路邊飛馳而過的景物也稍嫌單薄。家用版把8人連線的大型電玩機台簡化成單純的分割畫面雙人同樂。增加耐玩度的是額外的冒險模式，乍看像是附加內容，但實際上是在PC Engine眾多賽車遊戲中選擇本作的一大理由。
comment_en: A key component to enjoying these sort of games is for the track to   animate smoothly and the car should handle exceptionally well. The   PCE/Turbo version does these better than any other racer I’ve played on   the system. However, like the arcade version, the sparse offering of   billboards and road signs aren’t going to win any pixel art awards. They   whiz by very nicely for non-scaling graphics, there just isn’t much to   see. The stereo music and sound effects were adequate and quite   good when compared to the mono output of the 8-bit systems popular at   the time, but there was nothing ground breaking. What made this   game so endearing is the silly quest mode. It is a JRPG where a squat   character walks across a simple grid map and fights random battles   (Dragon Quest).  However, the battles are R.C. car races for money and   prestige. While the overhead quest looks like the most basic of early   8-bit games, the race graphics are quite good and spark the feeling of a   Go-Pro camera following your vehicle. You use your prize money to   upgrade the tires, fuel and ramp capabilities (wing) ensuring continued   wins. This upgrade feature is well balanced and succeeds where Victory Run did not. As   an added nod to this title’s playability, my sister was not a game   player.  But, I handed her the controls to Final Lap Twin’s RPG game and   she got so addicted, she played it until she beat it! W. Eric Myers
comment_zh: 這類遊戲好不好玩的關鍵在於賽道是否動得流暢、車子操控是否夠好。PCE／Turbo版這兩點都比我在這台主機上玩過的任何賽車遊戲做得更好。不過和大型電玩版一樣，路邊看板和路標都很稀少，絕對拿不到什麼像素美術獎。以非縮放圖形來說算是跑得蠻順的，就只是沒什麼好看的。立體聲音樂與音效算是夠用，跟當時主流8位元主機的單聲道輸出比起來算相當不錯，但沒什麼突破性的表現。讓這款遊戲令人難忘的就是那個傻氣的冒險模式。這是一款JRPG，矮胖角色在簡易格子地圖上行走、遇到隨機戰鬥（勇者鬥惡龍式）。不過戰鬥是為了金錢與名聲而舉辦的遙控車賽車。俯視冒險看起來像是最基本的早期8位元遊戲，但賽車場景畫面相當不錯，像是有GoPro相機跟著車子跑一樣。你用獎金升級輪胎、燃料與跳台能力（尾翼）來確保連勝。這套升級系統平衡得很好，比《Victory Run》成功太多。還有個能證明本作耐玩度的例子：我姊姊本來不是玩家，但我把Final Lap Twin的RPG模式交給她後，她著迷到一口氣玩到破關！W. Eric Myers
wiki_extract_en: Final Lap Twin (ファイナルラップツイン, Fainaru Rappu Tsuin) is a hybrid racing/role-playing game developed by Nova and published by Namco. Released for the Japanese PC Engine in 1989 and the North American TurboGrafx-16 in 1990, it is a spin-off to the 1987 arcade game Final Lap.

title_jp: ファイナルマッチテニス
overview_en: Tennis action game with standard modes such as Exhibition (single match), World Tour (career) and training stages.
overview_zh: 網球動作遊戲，具備標準模式，包括表演賽（單場）、世界巡迴（職業生涯）與訓練關卡。
wiki_extract_en:
Final Match Tennis (ファイナルマッチテニス) is a 1991 tennis video game that was developed and published by Human Entertainment exclusively for the PC Engine.
A similar game with improved graphics titled Final Match Tennis Ladies was included in Human Sports Festival, a sports compilation that contains three previously unreleased titles. Two standalone follow-ups were also released, 1994's Super Final Match Tennis for the Super Famicom and the 1996 PlayStation title Hyper Final Match Tennis.

title_jp: ファイナルソルジャー
overview_en: Another  shoot 'em up in Hudson’s Soldier series. This time you can set up different weapon styles at the beginning (if your preference is for homing missiles instead of forward-firing, for example) while weapons are switched throughout the game with the usual coloured pick-ups. Turbo Views Review
overview_zh: Hudson的Soldier系列射擊遊戲新作。這次在開場可以設定不同的武器風格（例如偏好追蹤飛彈而非前向火力），遊戲途中則透過熟悉的彩色強化道具切換武器。Turbo Views評測。

overview_en: Ikari Warriors/Red Alert style game where you run up the screen shooting the buggery out of various soldiers and armoured vehicles that stand in your way. Also contains a shoot 'em up stage to add a tiny bit of variety. Turbo Views Review
overview_zh: 《怒：Ikari Warriors》／《Red Alert》風格的遊戲，玩家往畫面上方推進，把擋路的各種士兵與裝甲車輛轟個稀爛。還加入了一段射擊關卡，增添少許變化。Turbo Views評測。
wiki_extract_en: Final Zone II is a run and gun video game created by Wolf Team and published by Telenet Japan for the TurboGrafx-16 on CD-ROM in Japan on March 23, 1990. It was later ported to the TurboGrafx-16 CD add-on in North America later that year by NEC. It is the official sequel to Final Zone.

title_jp: ファイプロ女子 憧夢超女大戦 全女 vs JWP
overview_en: Isometric sports game featuring Japanese female wrestlers.
overview_zh: 以日本女子摔角選手為主題的等角視角運動遊戲。
wiki_extract_en: Fire Pro Wrestling World is a professional wrestling video game published by Spike Chunsoft. The game was released on Steam on December 19, 2017, and for PlayStation 4 on August 9, August 28 and September 28, 2018, in Japan, the United States and Europe, respectively. The game is part of the Fire Pro Wrestling series.

title_jp: ファイヤープロレスリングコンビネーションタッグ
overview_en: Single or multi-player wrestling action game.
overview_zh: 單人或多人的摔角動作遊戲。
wiki_extract_en:
Fire Pro Wrestling (ファイヤープロレスリング, Faiyā Puro Resuringu) is a professional wrestling video game series originating from Japan, started in 1989 by Human Entertainment, and currently developed and owned by Spike Chunsoft. The series is distinguished by its grappling system, which is primarily based on timed button presses and strategy. Another signature feature of the series is its Edit mode, a character creation feature with many options to customize appearances, wrestling moves and character artificial intelligence behavior.
Unlike many other pro wrestling games, most Fire Pro games are not licensed by any major professional wrestling promotion, but feature likenesses of real-life wrestlers under different names. Games in the series generally utilize 2-D sprite-based graphics, with some later games incorporating 3D graphical elements. Most of the titles in the Fire Pro Wrestling series have originally been released in Japan, although some of the games have seen releases in North America and worldwide.

title_jp: ファイヤープロレスリング 2nd BOUT
overview_en: Isometric wrestling game sequel.
overview_zh: 等角視角摔角遊戲續作。

title_jp: ファイヤープロレスリング3 Legend BOUT
overview_en: Third in the wrestling series.
overview_zh: 摔角系列第三作。

title_jp: フラッシュハイダース
overview_en: Street Fighter style fighting game including versus mode, story mode and training mode to build up your character.
overview_zh: 快打旋風（Street Fighter）風格格鬥遊戲，包含對戰模式、故事模式，以及可強化角色的訓練模式。
wiki_extract_en:
Flash Hiders (Japanese: フラッシュハイダース) is a fighting video game developed and published in Japan by Right Stuff in 1993 for the PC Engine Super CD-ROM² console.
Flash Hiders was followed by a 1995 sequel titled Battle Tycoon: Flash Hiders SFX, which was also released exclusively in Japan, but developed for the Super Famicom.

title_jp: フォゴットンワールド
